attributes.class: login-class ng-untouched ng-pristine ng-valid ion-untouched ion-pristine ion-valid sc-ion-input-md-h sc-ion-input-md-s md input-label-placement-start in-item hydrated
ion-button-style md button button-round button-solid ion-activatable ion-focusable hydrated

reviewSummary.summary: The draft includes the primary login controls but contains duplicate password elements, unstable locators, decorative noise, and missing navigation and validation elements required for complete workflow automation coverage.

issues.message: Two separate elements represent the same password control, creating ambiguity for downstream automation generation.
issues.suggested_fix: Retain only the stable password component using locator id=password and remove the duplicate native input using id=ion-input-1.

issues.message: Locator id=ion-input-0 appears dynamically generated and may be unstable across executions.
issues.suggested_fix: Use a stable business-facing locator such as xpath=//input[@placeholder='User Name'].

issues.message: Locator id=ion-input-1 appears dynamically generated and brittle.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this duplicate element and retain the stable locator id=password.

issues.message: The element name is overly generic and does not clearly communicate the field purpose.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename the retained control to password_textbox or password_field.

issues.message: The background_content element is decorative layout structure and does not provide meaningful automation value.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the background_content element from the approved page model.

issues.message: The button locator depends entirely on visible text and may become brittle if UI text changes.
issues.suggested_fix: Use a more resilient locator such as xpath=//ion-button[contains(normalize-space(.),'SIGN IN') or @text='Login'].

issues.message: Back button required by the workflow and acceptance criteria is missing from the page model.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a reusable back_button navigation element using a stable locator.

issues.message: Home button required by the workflow and acceptance criteria is missing from the page model.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a reusable home_button navigation element using a stable locator.

issues.message: Authentication failure validation is required but no generic failed authentication message element is modeled.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a generic authentication_failed_message element using a visible failure-feedback locator.

issues.message: The workflow explicitly states that separate required-field validation messages should not appear, but the current model does not support validating this negative condition.
issues.suggested_fix: Model only the generic failed authentication message and avoid adding separate required-field validation elements.

issues.message: The retained ion-input password component is more accurately classified as a password field rather than a generic textbox.
issues.suggested_fix: Classify the element as password or password_field.

approved_elements.name: username_textbox
approved_elements.description: Username input field used for login authentication.

approved_elements.name: password_textbox
approved_elements.description: Masked password input field used for login authentication.

approved_elements.name: sign_in_button
approved_elements.description: Submits the entered credentials for authentication.

approved_elements.name: back_button
approved_elements.description: Returns the user from the login page to the home page.

approved_elements.name: home_button
approved_elements.description: Navigates the user back to the home page from the login view.

approved_elements.name: authentication_failed_message
approved_elements.description: Displays the generic failed authentication message for all unsuccessful login attempts.
